calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-29-2015 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-01-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RICHARD C. BENNETT, ESQ. of Bennett Johnson, Tel No. 510-444-5020 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Deputy City Attorney DAVID A. DELBON of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415-554-3962 appearing for the Defendant City and County of San Francisco;
Atty. WAYNE COLLINS, ESQ. of Toschi, Sidran, Collins & Doyle, Tel No. 415-835-3400 appearing for Defendant Donald MacDonald.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denoed. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production per CCP 437c. Gov't Code secs. 830.4 and 830.8 do not bar this action because lack of signage/signals is merely one component of the alleged defective condition of public property. In addition, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the condition was a trap. Defendant fails to show that the design immunity defense of Gov't Code sec. 830.6 applies. The Woo declaration does not sufficiently establish discretionary approval of the design, including the lag time between when northbound and southbound traffic stop. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether a dangerous condition existed, and whether defendant had actual and/or constructive notice of the alleged defects. Defendant created the timing for the lights. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
